title: Rhizanthes lowii
taxonomy: Rhizanthes lowii is a genus of flowering plants in the family Eulophiaceae. The classification of Rhizanthes lowii places it within the monocot order Asparagales. This genus consists of a single species, Rhizanthes lowii, which is native to the rainforests of Borneo. The species was first discovered in 1897 and is considered one of the most unusual orchids due to its unique habit of flowering only once in its lifetime. Rhizanthes lowii is a critically endangered species due to its limited range and habitat destruction.

intro: Rhizanthes lowii, also known as the 'Tongue Orchid', is a rare and unique species of orchid native to the rainforests of Borneo. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rhizanthes lowii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
